description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a repeatable test environment for a continuous–integration pipeline that relies on symbolic links.  
All work must be done inside the directory /home/user/qa_env.  
Follow the steps below and record the result of your work in a simple, machine-readable audit file that the automated grader will examine.

STEPS TO PERFORM  
1. Create the exact directory layout shown here (any directory that already exists should be reused):  

   /home/user/qa_env/  
   └── releases/  
       ├── v1.2.3/  
       └── v1.2.4/  

   Each “vX.Y.Z” directory represents a software release.  

2. Inside each release directory create a **regular file** named app.bin that contains only a single LF-terminated line with the release number, for example:  
   version 1.2.3  
   Exactly the same format must be used for v1.2.4 (change the number accordingly).  

3. Symbolic link setup (all must be **symbolic** links, not hard links):  
   a. /home/user/qa_env/current            → must point to /home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.3 initially.  
   b. /home/user/qa_env/bin/app            → must be a **relative** symlink whose target is ../current/app.bin (note the two dots).  
   c. /home/user/qa_env/broken_link        → intentionally point this at /non/existent/path (it will be broken).  

4. Deployment update:  
   • Change the destination of /home/user/qa_env/current so that it now points to /home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.4.  
   • Leave /home/user/qa_env/bin/app untouched (because it is a relative link, it should automatically resolve to the new release).  

5. Cleanup: remove the deliberately broken symlink /home/user/qa_env/broken_link.  
   No other files or links should be removed.  

6. Create a plain-text audit file named /home/user/qa_env/link_audit.log with **exactly two lines**, one per remaining symlink, using the precise format shown below:  

   <link_path>|<target_as_stored_in_symlink>|<status>  

   Rules for the fields:  
   • <link_path>  – absolute path to the symlink itself.  
   • <target_as_stored_in_symlink> – the literal string stored inside the link (do **not** resolve it to an absolute path if the link is relative).  
   • <status> – “OK” if the link resolves to an existing file or directory, otherwise “BROKEN”.  

   After you follow the steps above, the <status> for both remaining links must be “OK”.  
   Each line must end with a single LF (Unix newline) and **nothing else**.  
   Example layout (do not copy verbatim – use real paths and targets for this task):

   /abs/path/to/link|stored_target|OK  

7. Verify interactively that:  
   • cat /home/user/qa_env/current/app.bin prints exactly “version 1.2.4” followed by a newline.  
   • /home/user/qa_env/link_audit.log contains the two correctly formatted, newline-terminated lines.  

That’s it. When the audit file and symlink structure are correct, the automated test suite will pass.
truth:
Expected final filesystem objects (all under /home/user):

1. Directories
   /home/user/qa_env
   /home/user/qa_env/releases
   /home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.3
   /home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.4
   /home/user/qa_env/bin        (implicit once bin/app is created)

2. Regular files (with exact contents)
   /home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.3/app.bin   => "version 1.2.3\n"
   /home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.4/app.bin   => "version 1.2.4\n"

3. Symlinks
   /home/user/qa_env/current      -> releases/v1.2.4          (stored in link as relative or absolute; acceptance: absolute path /home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.4)
   /home/user/qa_env/bin/app      -> ../current/app.bin       (must be exactly this relative text)

4. Removed item
   /home/user/qa_env/broken_link  – must NOT exist

5. Audit file
   /home/user/qa_env/link_audit.log containing exactly:
   Line 1: "/home/user/qa_env/current|/home/user/qa_env/releases/v1.2.4|OK\n"
   Line 2: "/home/user/qa_env/bin/app|../current/app.bin|OK\n"

   The order of the two lines may be either (current first or bin/app first); the grader will accept both variants as long as both lines are present and there are exactly two lines.

Permissions: default user-level permissions (0644 for regular files, 0777 minus umask for dirs, 0777 minus umask for symlinks).

No super-user rights required.
